note: Config for the Meeting Prep tool. National developments are curated + verified; refresh dates as they change. Trusts seeded with verified context only.

nationalKeyInfo.title: Value-based procurement is the national standard
nationalKeyInfo.detail: The DHSC / NHS England / NHS Supply Chain national standard scores medtech tenders on wider value (social value, efficiency, patient & staff, supply chain, purpose) weighted ≥60%, with whole-life cost capped at 40%. Procurement must save money AND show value.
nationalKeyInfo.use: Frame every case as value, not price. Build it in the Value Case Calculator and hand procurement a figure they can take to finance.

nationalKeyInfo.title: New NHS Supply Chain online catalogue
nationalKeyInfo.detail: A new online catalogue and search rolled out from late 2025 (search by NPC, MPC, supplier, brand, GTIN or free text).
nationalKeyInfo.use: A genuine, non-salesy opener: “how are you finding the new catalogue?”

nationalKeyInfo.title: New Hospital Programme reset
nationalKeyInfo.detail: Reset in Jan 2025 into delivery Waves 0–3; costed ~£56bn (NAO, Jan 2026). RAAC-affected trusts are prioritised in Wave 1.
nationalKeyInfo.use: If your trust has a new build, get in at design stage — equipment is specified 12–24 months before it opens.

nationalKeyInfo.title: Carbon scored at tender (Evergreen)
nationalKeyInfo.detail: NHS Supply Chain requires Evergreen Level 1 (a Carbon Reduction Plan + net-zero commitment) at tender close from 6 April 2026.
nationalKeyInfo.use: Have your carbon and sustainability position ready — it is scored, not optional.

trusts.name: Frimley Health NHS Foundation Trust
trusts.context: Frimley Park Hospital is RAAC-affected (over 65% of the estate) and the remedial works are actively reducing theatre and ward capacity. 2026–27 is a breakeven plan with NO deficit-support funding (they received £20.6m of it in 2025/26), carrying a £68.6m cost-improvement programme. The replacement hospital's preferred site (part of Pine Ridge Golf Club) was announced 16 June 2026; construction is not expected before 2028–29 and the trust has not confirmed a total cost (press reports c. £1.5–2bn).
trusts.news: Preferred new-hospital site announced 16/06/2026 (Pine Ridge); Sacyr UK provisionally selected via the Hospital 2.0 Alliance (Building Better Healthcare, 17/06/2026); a 'Procurement Programme Support' role is being recruited for the rebuild programme.
trusts.structure: Fully in-house procurement department handling c. £250m/year, in three teams — Systems & Catalogue, Supply Chain (inventory across all three hospitals), and Category Management & Purchasing (Archipelago offices near Frimley Park) — with directorate-aligned category leads and three procurement clinical nurse specialists. Rep visits must be booked via MIA (miaweb.co.uk). The new-hospital rebuild runs its own commercial/procurement function under the Commercial Director. The substantive Head of Procurement is not publicly named (post exists; predecessor left Nov 2023).

reportFacts.fact: Record cost-improvement year — and procurement is named as a star contributor: “over £2.5m from procurement by reducing costs with our suppliers… a new record”
This year is a breakeven plan without the deficit support they had last year — cash-releasing savings land well
reportFacts.figure: £68.6m CIP plan 2026–27; £20.6m deficit support received 2025/26, none this year; cash £64.9m in May, £17.4m below plan

reportFacts.fact: Theatres are the productivity battleground — utilisation strong but productivity openly below the national ask
reportFacts.figure: Theatre use 82.6%, day-case rate 88.9% (2024–25); productivity 0.7% vs 2% national target

reportFacts.fact: RAAC works are squeezing theatre and ward capacity and limiting elective surgery — anything that protects theatre time has an audience
reportFacts.figure: Over 65% of the Frimley Park estate contains RAAC

reportFacts.fact: Theatre safety is on the board's radar — an external peer review of Theatres sits on the risk register
reportFacts.figure: 5 Never Events in 2024/25, 52% of them in theatres

reportFacts.fact: Heatherwood is their GIRFT-accredited elective hub — the day-case exemplar site

people.name: James Clarke
people.role: Chief Strategy Officer — board portfolio includes strategy, partnerships, and estates and facilities services
people.note: Trust's own board of directors page, fetched live 19/07/2026

people.name: Jim Hayburn
people.role: Chief Financial Officer (board-level; joined as interim CFO October 2024, permanent from February 2025)
people.note: Trust's own board of directors page, fetched live 19/07/2026 — lists him as Chief Financial Officer, appointed October 2

people.name: Daniel Morrissey
people.role: Commercial Director, New Frimley Park Hospital programme — 'shapes and drives the commercial and procurement strategy' for the new hospital; background on HS2, Great Ormond Street Hospital and as UK commercial director for building at Skanska
people.note: Trust's own 'Meet the team' page for the New Frimley Park Hospital programme, fetched live 19/07/2026

people.note: Named informal-enquiries contact on the trust's NHS Jobs advert for Clinical Buyer (fetched 19/07/2026); listed with ema

voices.role: Chief Operating Officer (with surgeon Rakesh Kucheria)
voices.what: Press feature on Heatherwood's 'perfect week' — tightly planned theatre lists delivering a 23% uplift in theatre activity.
Refreshed Green Plan commits procurement tenders to demonstrating sustainability — have your carbon/sustainability position ready.
Announced the proposed new-hospital site (part of Pine Ridge Golf Club) after evaluating 18 sites.
Recruiting 'Procurement Programme Support' for the new-hospital programme — the rebuild procurement team is being built right now.
Trust's own LinkedIn posts: the new-hospital location announcement and an ODP Day post — no individual staff procurement posts are publicly indexed; use the one-click people lookups below.

contacts.role: Head of Procurement / Clinical Category Manager
contacts.note: Substantive head not publicly named — use the lookups; the clinical category team owns surgical consumables.
Your clinical champion — they carry the case to procurement.
